title: LATAM Clinical Trial Recruitment Gap
problem: Clinical trial recruitment in LATAM is slow and inefficient. No centralized patient registry for trials. Manual chart review to find eligible patients. Recruitment delays cost pharma millions per day. Diverse populations (important for trial validity) are underrepresented. Brazil's SUS has 150M patients but no easy way to identify trial candidates.
solution: Clinical trial patient matching platform. Automated screening of EMR data against trial criteria. Patient registry for trial interest. Secure matching with trial sites. Recruitment analytics. Integration with hospital research departments. Compliance with trial regulations.
technology: EMR data extraction, trial eligibility algorithms, patient portal, secure messaging, analytics, GCP compliance
target_customer: Pharmaceutical companies, CROs (contract research organizations), hospital research departments, clinical trial sites
traction_signals: Pharma shifting trials to LATAM (cost, diversity)
SUS patient population attractive for trials
Proven models (Deep 6 AI, TriNetX in US)
regulatory_notes: Clinical trial regulations (GCP), IRB/ethics board requirements, patient consent
varden_fit: MEDIUM FIT - Niche but high-value. Pharma pays well for recruitment acceleration. Could start with Brazil (largest patient population) and expand.
notes: Clinical trial tech is specialized but lucrative. Brazil's SUS population is massive untapped resource for trials. Pharma will pay for access.
